summary: Stripped the embedded GitHub token from this fork's origin remote; gh set up as credential helper.
Created the Linear initiative 'Personal AI Platform' (top-level — this workspace has no sub-initiative support) and connected it as this project's tracker.

Scaffolded native governance: CLAUDE.md, .claude/agents/ (pm, de, da, code-reviewer, devils-advocate, corpus-steward), .claude/skills/ (log-activity, log-tracker, close-task, council, new-doc), FRAMEWORK.md, TODO.md, project_chronicle.md, context_snapshot.md. Written to avoid the plugin-dependency problem found in an earlier project.

Diagnosed and fixed a git push 403 to origin (Zuus89/omnigent): a fine-grained PAT had read-only Contents permission on this repo (gh api reads succeeded, git-receive-pack was denied). Fixed by deleting the PAT and re-authenticating gh via OAuth device flow (scope: repo). Also fixed branch.main.remote, which was pointed at upstream instead of origin. Pushed the 6 pending commits.
Confirmed by the user: the embedded GitHub token originally found in origin's URL has been revoked on GitHub's side. This closes the last open flag from V1's close.

Task kb-three-tier: Step-1 brief captured and design direction approved — three KB tiers as three kinds of git repos (kb-global / kb-ws-<company> hosted under that company's identity / per-repo docs/knowledge_base), uniform note frontmatter, curator promotion flow (never silent), SilverBullet as human access layer (one composed Space per workspace, global mounted read-only), secrets never in KB. Task runs its own full-lifecycle session later; blocked by the Workspace-layer task.

Task secrets-manager: Step-1 brief captured — workspace-scoped secrets manager on the VPS, manageable from the platform's own UI (not terminal); engine-not-product principle locked (proven manager as engine, our UI over its API); client-owned credentials stay in client systems (hypothesis pending deep-research report); migration from gitignored .env files in scope. Depends on the Workspace-layer task.

Task workspace-layer: Step-1 brief approved — build the Workspace entity as a real identity/credential boundary (workspace = client company; git identity, cloud credentials, MCP grants, filesystem; nothing crosses by default; one 'personal' workspace today, second must be trivial). Foundation for kb-three-tier and secrets-manager. Full 10-step lifecycle starts this session.

Task workspace-layer: task-start milestone. Phase 2's first task begins its full 10-step lifecycle; Step-2 da profiling launched (5 parallel readers + synthesis). Linear comment NOT posted — connector session expired, needs re-auth via claude.ai settings; post retroactively and append the comment id in a follow-up record when restored.

Task workspace-layer: Step-2 state profile complete (5 parallel da readers + da synthesis, read-only, exact evidence, HEAD 9e3942b8). Key findings: no entity above session; project = omni_project label only (different repos per session is definitional); 'workspace' means two unrelated things (per-session path + dormant workspace_id tenant partition key on all 12 tables, never activated in this fork); credentials are process-global env + per-user keychain, no per-scope mechanism; backend never sets git identity; MCP grants per-session-agent, no persistent store; no /v1/workspaces API. Brief premise confirmed by every dimension.

Task workspace-layer: deferred Linear milestone comment posted retroactively (task-start, originally queued 2026-07-14 while the connector was down after the claude.ai account switch). Cross-references the 2026-07-14 milestone record that carried tracker_comment_id null.

Task workspace-layer: Step-3 spec expanded from a 3-candidate x 3-judge design panel. Winner: platform-registry-hybrid (121/150) — workspace = one omnigent-host container holding all identity (creds, git, filesystem, MCP env), single shared server stripped of credentials, workspaces.yaml registry in the fork, compose/env in vps-infra, pre-planned escalation stages (2a switcher, 2b server-side pinning, 3 partition activation with fail-open fix). activate-partition lost on the fail-open workspace_scope hazard (103/150); container-per-workspace lost on resource fit (107/150), kept as single-client escape valve. 7 open decisions posed to the human before freeze.

Task workspace-layer: the human ruled all 7 open decisions question-by-question. Stage 1 satisfies the locked isolation constraint; escalation triggers are BINDING; probe-(a) failure pulls Stage 2b into the immediate successor; registry SSOT rule adopted; data-at-rest posture must be resolved BEFORE the first client workspace (new binding gate); personal history stays, new sessions only; extension-only switcher interim, priority = speed to first POC.

Task workspace-layer: Step-4 alpha test designed from the spec alone (17 mandatory binary checks / 8 groups; timed ws-test dry-run centerpiece; non-mutating isolation proofs via mount+hash-inequality; binary PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED rule). Status draft — seals at Step 6. da flagged pm items: pin BASE commit at seal, ratify web/ inclusion in the governance diff, decide agents-table lint gating; Stage-2b cross-host enforcement deliberately untested per human ruling 1.

Task workspace-layer: transitory close mid-Step-5. Done: brief, da profile, panel-based spec + 7 human rulings, alpha test (draft), devils-advocate review (2 BLOCKERs + 5 MAJORs). Pending: de feasibility review (2 attempts died — rerun fresh, read-only, server-probe consent still pending), Step-6 resolution (likely /council on re-centering scope to the code-server plane) + 3 da ratifications, freeze + seal. Secrets research paused (resumable wf_7943ab35-6a7). vps-infra architect prompt delivered, response pending. Snapshot + TODO synced.

Task workspace-layer: de feasibility review complete (read-only, code-analysis probes). Verdict FEASIBLE-WITH-CHANGES. Probe (a) tunnel token inert BUT session-create authorizes host by owner-equality only, and all workspaces share one owner => control plane has ZERO cross-workspace isolation (triggers human ruling 3: pull Stage 2b forward). (b) env allowlist confirmed clean per-container; spec probed wrong file (managed_hosts is the unused sandbox path). (c) workspace_scope read-side filtering confirmed wired (70 sites in conversation_store), fails-open at 0 as ruled. CRITICAL: owner-scoping != workspace-scoping. MAJOR: runtime MCP-edit writes inline secrets into commingled agents table (confirms devils-advocate M5). Credential strip feasible. Confirms devils-advocate BLOCKER1/M4/M5/M7/M8 from the code side.

Task workspace-layer: vps-infra architecture ruling received (authored by the vps-infra session, carried by the human). A2 topology: single code-server, convention-based isolation (filesystem + git includeIf incl. credential.helper scoping + path-scoped credential files, never container env vars); A1 dedicated container = per-workspace escalation only; Omnigent server has NO role in the Workspace entity (C1/C2); Anthropic key stays global; transcripts same convention scoping, CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR-style per-invocation scoping flagged for product-side verification; mem_limit 4g / cpus 1.5 / ~3-4 concurrent Claude processes ceiling; wide multi-agent workflows do NOT belong on this box; ephemeral-server probe DECLINED as moot. Resolves devils-advocate B1/M4/M5 by design; B2 (amending locked plan text) explicitly routed back as a /council call.

Task workspace-layer: /council on amending the locked Phase-2 isolation text. Positions: pm AMEND, de+da AMEND-WITH-CONDITIONS. Devils-advocate: 2 new BLOCKERs (threat model false — resident prompt-injection vector runs with coder uid daily; reopen triggers unobservable by construction) + unscored A2-prime option (per-workspace Unix uids, kernel-enforced, ~A2 cost). RESOLUTION SPLITS: Decision 1 (decouple Workspace from Omnigent server) = recommend enact, spec-level, to human now; Decision 2 (A2-as-default locked-text amendment) = NOT YET, gated on: score A2-prime vs A2/A1 (routed to vps-infra with the attack), threat-model restatement, one observable trigger, CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR full-state verification, A1 capacity stated as <=1. Council doc has explicit reopen criteria.

Task workspace-layer: council missing-item 3 RESOLVED. CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR isolation verified two ways: docs confirm .credentials.json lives under the config dir on Linux (code.claude.com/docs/en/authentication.md); empirical probe on this box (claude 2.1.207, scratch config dir, zero API calls, cleaned up) shows 'Not logged in' under a scratch dir while global ~/.claude/.credentials.json exists — the CLI treats CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR as its whole state base. Cost stated honestly: per-workspace login ceremony. Remaining council gate: A2-prime scoring + threat-model restatement + observable trigger + zombie-stack ruling — handoff doc written for the vps-infra architect.

Collaboration vision captured (brief-grade, mid-workspace-layer): the platform must scale to collaborative use — a second person with the same software joins a shared project (repo), live mutual visibility, each using THEIR OWN harness credentials (Claude Code/Copilot/Antigravity). pm mapping: two axes (client isolation vs collaboration), the Omnigent server IS the existing engine for the vision (multi-user, session_permissions, live status, 11 harnesses, credentials travel with each user's host). Consequences: council Decision-1 reframed (decouple != discard; de's keep-the-stack + reserved workspace_id conditions upgraded to strategy); vps-infra follow-up prompt question 3 revised BEFORE delivery; plan.md amendment candidate pending human wording approval.

Task workspace-layer: the human APPROVED council Decision 1 with its collaboration reframing — the Workspace entity (client isolation) is built WITHOUT the Omnigent server, and the server (the fork's own program running on the user's VPS, clarified: no third-party involved) is PRESERVED as the future collaboration engine; each registry entry reserves a workspace_id from day one. Spec re-centering deferred until council Decision 2 (isolation model) lands, to rewrite once. Human also confirmed understanding: stop-but-preserve is the likely stack disposition, final call with the vps-infra architect (revised prompt question 3).

Task workspace-layer: vps-infra ruling 2 (post-council, live-measured). Q1: A2-prime ADOPT but INDIVISIBLE — per-workspace uids + DELETE the blanket NOPASSWD:ALL sudo (which voids any kernel boundary; reverses ruling-1's keep-sudo call) + claudeProcessWrapper privilege-drop treated and tested as a security control; group-based layout (owner ws-X, group coder, secrets 600) keeps the editor working; hardens the agent path, not human terminals; needs a custom image layer (useradd absent). Q2: threat model RE-DECLARED — injection in scope today, 'I was wrong'; exposure sentence goes in the plan verbatim until mitigated; pure A2 has NO observable trigger (council requirement unmet). Q3: stack stop-but-preserve (compose stop, never down -v) — strong reason: injected agents reach 127.0.0.1:8000 via host networking. Q4: A1 ceiling confirmed <=1; A1 = isolation not capacity; 2 vCPU binds before RAM. E2 (mem_limit/cpus) overdue, now a containment control. All 5 council Decision-2 gates cleared.

Task workspace-layer: council Decision 2 RESOLVED. Human chose A2-prime (real per-workspace kernel isolation via Unix uids) as the default model, accepting the indivisible bundle (uids + delete blanket NOPASSWD sudo + tested launch wrapper). Added the binding master-key principle: root belongs ONLY to the vps-infra architect at host level; inside the code-server container nobody holds root, not coder nor any agent — infra changes become technically impossible from inside the container, routed to the architect. Consequence: the sudo chown workaround for provisioning defects goes away by design; durable host-side fix only. All council gates cleared; both decisions enactable. Next: pm rewrites the spec, then presents the exact plan.md amendment for human approval.
